string 2: Damn! I'm so useless like this...

Um, what do you mean?
We defeated the enemy, didn't we?
We're normally much more powerful than this!
It must be because our partners aren't by our side...

I understand being worried about your partners. I doubt I'd
be very strong, either, if Inori weren't right here with me.

Our partners hold a special destiny. They work together 
with us, their partner Digimon, to protect the world.

We have faced many crises before, but I never expected
anything like this to ever happen!
This must be... Shinjuku, when it was peaceful.

Akashic Backdoors use space-time disturbances to
pry open pathways to different points in time and space.

This creates isolated fragments of space-time called
"Akashic debris," which is what you're looking at right now.
This place...
Dad...
Seems like he can't hear us.
Did you just say, "Dad"?

Yes, the one wearing glasses...
That's my dad.
I see...
So, your last name is "Misono"?
What?!
My name is... Kyoko Kuremi.

My father worked for the public safety office.
He used to tell me about your father from time to time.
That's my father right there.

Y-You don't say...
That's some coincidence.

There's nothing more for us here. 
Let's go.
...Looking at his face really pisses me off.
Huh?
To me, he's just someone who used to be my father.

Anyway, let's keep moving.
We need to find Parallelmon before it's too late.

"The higher-ups are the same as when you were there.
Meetings after meetings, but no real conclusions or results."

"It's always the people on the ground who suffer
from their ambiguous orders."
"Heh."
"What's so funny?"

"I guess we haven't changed much, either. We've been
complaining about the brass for as long as I can remember."

"But now you've gotten promoted. If word gets out that you're
discussing internal matters with civvies, you'll be fired."

"Well, in that case, you can just hire me here. I'd be a
better private eye than you could ever hope to be."
Those two seem really close.

That look... I don't think I've ever seen him
make that face in front of family.
Kyoko...?

It doesn't look like there's anything else
to investigate here. Let's move on.

Countless fragments of space-time lie buried
beyond an Akashic Backdoor.

Apparently, some of that debris was taken
from times and places related to you.

While not very strong, there's a force that draws beings back
to the parts of space-time that they were cut off from.

As a side effect of that, you're all basically acting like
magnets, drawing specific bits of debris toward you.

I suppose you could say that's the mechanism at work
behind the phenomena you're seeing now.
"I really... don't want to fight..."

"Just because I was born a Titan, I've been
forced into a battle I have no desire to fight."

"I just want to live a normal life.
Please, you have to believe me..."
"Thank you."

"You foolish human.
You'll serve as my stepping stone to greatness!"
"Hahahahaha!"

More than likely, one of you has drawn in this scene.
Any guess as to who?

That Digimon... A Titan...
Wait. It can't be...

That attack on Captain Kuremi they mentioned... 
Could this be THAT incident...?
"Really? Who the hell is bothering me now...?!"
"Hey! I don't have time for—"

"Wh-What did you say?
The captain's been seriously injured?!"
Did... Digimon attack humans in your world?
Yeah... My father was hurt pretty badly by them.
I was young at the time, but I still remember it clearly.

I couldn't stop crying. They took him to the hospital,
and we didn't know if he was going to pull through.
The Backdoor Key has been verified.

"It seems I'll need physical therapy. Even with it, though,
there's no guarantee I'll ever regain full mobility."
"The doctor has already recommended that I retire."

"Well, if that's the extent of it, you're lucky. There was a moment
when it didn't seem like you were going to make it."
"...So, what're you planning on doing next?"

"Nothing in particular, really... All I want is to
continue to provide for my wife and daughter."

"How about becoming a private investigator?
I can introduce you to some clients."

"I'm just about to close down my business.
Feel free to take whatever you need."
"You mean you're getting out of the PI racket?"

"Yep. I figure it's time I go put an end to
the cause of all my loss and misery."

Your father was seriously hurt by a Digimon—so much so
that there were severe after-effects from his injuries.

As such, I imagine you must detest Digimon
with all of your being...
Yes, there's no point in trying to hide it.

To be honest, I'm really not fond of them,
to say the least. I'm sorry.
Kyoko... I...

Although there are Digimon who attack humans
like the ones just now... we are certainly not like that.

Together with our partners, we've saved the world multiple times.
Even now, the powerful bond this has formed between us holds true.

That's right. And there are lots of Digimon who
get along well with humans.

Yes, I suppose so... although that definitely
hasn't been my experience of them.

"Are you sure about this? Should you be sharing
such confidential information with an outsider?"
"I want you to come back, Captain."
"...That's 'former captain,' and I'm sorry."

"Your rehab is going well, right? 
You seem to be moving around pretty good..."

"I made a decision eight years ago to
protect the things that are important to me."

"You'll understand when you have
a family of your own someday."
The Backdoor Key has been verified.
"So, they haven't found Kuroi, either..."

"That's right, sir... I'm afraid we've lost
many of our old comrades."

"Some were caught up in the explosion over the government building.
Others died because of anomalous phenomena..."

"There are also lots of people who are still unaccounted for,
but there's one thing we can say for certain."

"Every one of them was doing all they could
to accomplish their mission."

"So I can't just sit around doing nothing.
Dr. Simmons and I are returning to Anomalous Affairs."

"The public safety office is barely even functional anymore,
but somebody has to do something..."
"Mr. Kuremi... No, Captain Kuremi..."
"...what are you going to do?"

So this is the world you live in...
I can see why you hate Digimon so much.
But there's something I don't understand...

Your eyes, when you're looking at your father...
they're so scary.

In fact, they're even scarier than when you're
looking at the Digimon you hate so much.
"So... you're really going?"

"Mom was crying, you know. I can't believe you're
going back to such a dangerous place at a time like this."

"People have given up already.
It's only a matter of time now before the world ends."

"That's why she wants to spend her final days
with her family... and so do I."
"But why...? Why...?"
Kyoko...
Like I said, he "used to be my father."
I imagine he's been sent to this place, too.

I heard he went to face Parallelmon
during their rampage in Shinjuku.
Then, after that, he disappeared.

He went to face Parallelmon?
He sure is a brave man.

You mean "stupid." Humans don't stand a chance
against something like that.

He probably forgot just how badly he was injured
by a Digimon...

Forgot how his daughter was crying by his side
in the hospital...
It makes me angry just thinking about it.
